results.content:
Follow

dsn_ai_network's profile picture

dsn\_ai\_network

How can AI better understand space and movement?   
  
A new paper introduces SpatialClaw, a training-free framework that helps vision-language models reason more effectively about objects, positions, and movement.   
  
Across 20 spatial-reasoning benchmarks, it achieved 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming a recent spatial agent by 11.2 percentage points.   
  
Read the paper:    
  
#ArtificialIntelligence #MultimodalAI #AIAgents

Instead, SpatialClaw gives the agent a persistent Python kernel with: - SAM3 for object segmentation - Depth Anything 3 for depth, camera geometry, and 3D point maps - NumPy / SciPy / Matplotlib for custom geometric computation and visualization The agent can write Python step-by-step, inspect intermediate masks / depth / 3D geometry, and revise its next action based on execution feedback. results.title: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning - Moonlight
results.content:
This paper introduces SpatialClaw, a training-free framework that rethinks the action interface for spatial reasoning by adopting code as the primary interaction medium. SpatialClaw operates through a stateful Python kernel, pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives. This design allows a VLM-backed agent to write and execute one Python cell per step, conditioned on all prior outputs, thereby enabling flexible composition and manipulation of perception results, and dynamic adaptation of the analysis based on intermediate text and visual observations, as well as problem demands.

Core Methodology:

SpatialClaw's methodology is built upon two main components: a persistent kernel workspace and a five-stage agentic loop. [...] Spatial Reasoning Loop: Code, Inspect, and Revise:
A five-stage outer loop coordinates the agent's actions, guided by a unified system prompt encoding general spatial reasoning principles.

`purpose`
`reasoning`
`next goal`
`code`
`print()`
`show()`
`ReturnAnswer()`

Evaluation and Results:

SpatialClaw was evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks, categorized into single-image, multi-view, video spatial & 4D, general spatial, and general video understanding tasks. It was tested with six open-source VLM backbones (Qwen3.5, Qwen3.6, Gemma4) ranging from 26B to 397B parameters, using a consistent configuration (same hyperparameters, system prompts, and perception tools) across all benchmarks and models.

Analysis and Insights: [...] Analysis and Insights:

`tools.Mask`
`tools.Geometry`
`scipy.spatial.KDTree`
`np.linalg.norm`
`np.dot`

In conclusion, SpatialClaw demonstrates that adopting code as a flexible, multi-turn action interface in a persistent Python kernel significantly enhances an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning, allowing for iterative inspection, composition, and revision of perception outputs. The results underscore the critical yet underexplored importance of action interface design in advancing spatial reasoning capabilities for VLMs.

results.title: Broken Cat Claw or Nail: What You Should Do If This Happens | Partners Animal Hospital Asheville
results.content:
Limping or Avoiding Using the Paw: If your cat is reluctant to use a specific paw, it could indicate a broken claw or nail.
 Visible Damage: Check for any obvious signs of a broken or split claw or nail.
 Bleeding: A broken claw or nail may bleed, especially if it has broken near the quick, the sensitive part of the nail containing blood vessels.
 Excessive Licking: Your cat may lick the affected paw more frequently in an attempt to soothe the pain.
 Swelling or Redness: The area around the broken claw or nail may appear swollen or red due to inflammation.

## Immediate Steps to Take for a Broken Cat Claw or Nail [...] american-shorthair-cat-laying-on-the-floor

When your cat has a broken claw or nail, it can be a distressing experience for both you and your feline friend. A broken cat claw can cause significant pain and may lead to further complications if not addressed promptly. In this article, we will discuss the causes of broken cat claws and broken cat nails, the signs to look for, and the steps you should take if your cat experiences these issues. If your cat suffers from a broken claw or broken cat nail, call us at Partners Animal Hospital Asheville at (828) 333-7356 or book an appointment online!

## Understanding Broken Cat Claws and Nails [...] ### Minimize Stress and Discomfort

Keep your cat calm and restrict their activity to prevent further injury. Provide a comfortable and quiet space for your cat to rest. Avoid letting your cat go outdoors or engage in activities that could exacerbate the injury.

## Seeking Veterinary Care for a Broken Cat Claw or Nail

ItŌĆÖs crucial to seek professional veterinary care for more severe cases of broken cat claws or broken cat nails. HereŌĆÖs what to expect when you take your cat to the vet:

### Professional Examination

A veterinarian will thoroughly examine your catŌĆÖs paw to assess the extent of the damage. They may recommend additional tests, such as X-rays, if they suspect underlying issues or more severe trauma.

### Treatment Options
